title: netrc credentials should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: netrc credentials detected in source code. The .netrc file format stores machine authentication credentials (machine, login, password) in plaintext, providing direct access to remote systems. Exposed .netrc entries allow unauthorized users to authenticate to servers, FTP sites, Git repositories, and other network resources. These credentials are typically used by tools like curl, wget, and git, making them highly sensitive. Store authentication credentials securely in encrypted vaults or environment variables instead of hardcoding .netrc format in source code.

netrc credentials string detected in configuration. Hardcoded .netrc format credentials in configuration files expose authentication details for remote machines. This format is commonly used by networking tools (curl, wget, git) to automatically authenticate to remote servers. Exposure allows attackers to impersonate your application when accessing external services, potentially leading to data breaches or service compromise. Use secure credential storage mechanisms instead.

netrc-style login and password pair detected. Credentials in 'login <user> password <pass>' format (common in .netrc files) expose authentication details for automated network operations. These credentials can be used by various tools (FTP clients, HTTP clients, version control systems) to access protected resources. Exposing this format allows unauthorized access to the specified services. Store credentials outside source code using environment variables or secret managers.

netrc password variable detected in source code. Hardcoded passwords in variables related to .netrc configuration expose authentication credentials for remote machines. The .netrc format is used by command-line tools to store authentication information, and exposing these passwords compromises access to multiple services. Rotate exposed credentials immediately and use secure credential storage.
